executive_summary: Build failure analysis for scib_demo repository on main branch (Attempt 3). Critical configuration error detected: GitHub Actions workflow file not found. The workflow dispatch endpoint returned HTTP 404, indicating that the .github/workflows/build.yml file does not exist in the repository or is not accessible with the current permissions. This prevents any build execution from occurring.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The GitHub Actions workflow dispatch API returned HTTP 404 Not Found when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates that the workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository or the authentication token lacks the necessary permissions to access it.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml is missing from the repository, or the GitHub token does not have sufficient permissions (workflow/actions scope) to access or trigger workflows in the loginkarun/scib_demo repository.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation, testing, and deployment processes.

steps: 2. Add build.yml workflow file with Maven/Gradle build configuration
3. Configure workflow_dispatch trigger to enable manual builds
4. Verify GitHub token has 'workflow' and 'actions:write' scopes
5. Test workflow dispatch API endpoint after file creation
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Workflow file creation requires code review and approval per enterprise DevOps standards. Token permissions must be validated against security policies.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Not Enabled or Accessible
errors.description: The 404 response may also indicate that GitHub Actions is not enabled for this repository, or the repository settings prevent workflow execution.
errors.error_message: Actions disabled or repository not accessible. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches
errors.root_cause: GitHub Actions may be disabled at the repository or organization level, or the repository visibility/permissions prevent Actions from running.
errors.impact: Even if workflow files exist, builds cannot execute if Actions is disabled. This requires administrative intervention to enable.

steps: 1. Navigate to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Verify 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows' is selected
3. Check organization-level Actions policies if applicable
4. Confirm repository is not archived or disabled
5. Validate token has admin or write access to repository settings
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Actions enablement requires repository admin privileges. Changes must be logged in audit trail per compliance requirements.

errors.title: Insufficient GitHub Token Permissions
errors.description: The GitHub personal access token used for API calls may lack the required 'workflow' scope needed to dispatch workflow runs.
errors.root_cause: The GitHub authentication token may not have the 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to trigger workflow_dispatch events via the GitHub API.
errors.impact: API calls to dispatch workflows will fail with 404 errors even if workflow files exist. This prevents automated build triggering and CI/CD pipeline execution.

steps: 1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Locate the authentication token used for API calls
4. Optionally enable 'actions:write' for full Actions API access
5. Save changes and update token in automation scripts
6. Test workflow dispatch API call with updated token
recommended_fix.compliance_notes: Token scope changes must be approved by security team. Token rotation and audit logging required per security policy.

next_steps: 2. IMMEDIATE: Verify GitHub Actions is enabled in repository settings
3. IMMEDIATE: Validate GitHub token has 'workflow' scope
4. Commit workflow file to main branch with proper Maven/Gradle configuration
5. Test workflow dispatch API endpoint after fixes applied
6. Re-run build automation after configuration corrections

compliance_audit.analysis_method: Automated error classification from build failure logs
